title: Regularization & Generalization
summary: Techniques to prevent overfitting and ensure the model generalizes beyond training data. Includes explicit methods (dropout, weight decay, L1/L2) and implicit (SGD noise, data augmentation, early stopping). Connects to pruning — regularization-driven sparsity is a production compression technique.

mathematical_core.l1: L_total = L_task + λ Σ|θ_i| — drives weights to zero (sparsity)
mathematical_core.l2: L_total = L_task + λ Σθ_i² — drives weights to be small (not zero)
mathematical_core.dropout: During training: randomly zero p fraction of activations. During inference: scale by (1-p).
mathematical_core.weight_decay: θ_{t+1} = (1-λη)θ_t - η·∇L — shrinks weights each step

enables: PRODUCTION.PRUNING — L1 regularization creates prunable weights
related: FOUNDATIONS.BATCHING_SGD — SGD noise is implicit regularization
